insights.dailyDesc: Based on your recent usage, we suggest charging {{kwh}} kWh today.
insights.dailyExplanation: AI analyzes your average daily consumption and applies seasonal corrections to suggest a charge that maintains stable SoC without unnecessary deep cycles.
insights.optimalDayDesc: Your driving patterns indicate this is the day with least vehicle usage or highest availability for slow charging, ideal for cell balancing.
insights.optimalWindowDesc: Based on your parking habits and weekly consumption, these windows offer enough time to complete your charge efficiently and economically.
insights.recommend_calibration: Calibration Required: LFP battery needs a slow charge to 100% regularly. Your {{kwh}} kWh/week consumption allows doing this overnight.
insights.recommend_offpeak: Smart Savings: Your estimated weekly consumption is {{kwh}} kWh. You can cover this perfectly using off-peak windows (or weekends) without affecting your routine.
insights.optimal_maintenance: Optimal Maintenance: For your {{kwh}} kWh/week consumption, we recommend slow charging. This maximizes battery lifespan and reduces thermal stress.
insights.comfortDesc: You rarely drop below {{soc}}% battery. You have room to space out your charges every {{days}} days.
insights.cityDesc: Low speed, high efficiency due to regenerative braking. Ideal for EVs.
insights.highwayDesc: High speed increases aerodynamic drag, significantly reducing range.
insights.mixedDesc: A balance of city and highway driving.

tips.city1: Use Eco mode for smoother acceleration

tips_hwy.hwy1: Keep speed under 110km/h for best range

tips_mixed.mixed1: Anticipate traffic to avoid harsh braking

settings.priceCalculatedNoData: No charge data available to calculate
settings.priceCalculatedNoCharges: Add charges to calculate the price automatically
settings.fuelPriceHint: Average fuel price for cost calculations (hybrid only)
settings.priceDynamicsHint: Uses the price of the most recent charge before the trip

modals.odometerDesc: Visual adjustment to correct the difference with the car's dashboard.

mfgDate.title: Manufacturing Date
mfgDate.desc: To accurately calculate the State of Health (SoH) of your LFP battery, we need to know when the car was manufactured.
mfgDate.instructions: Open the passenger door (sometimes the driver's) and look for a metal plate or black sticker on the door frame (the central pillar where the belt is anchored). On this plate, in addition to the VIN, the field 'Date of mfg' or 'Built date' usually appears with the format MM/YY.

thermalStress.title: Thermal Stress Factor
thermalStress.desc: Adjust the multiplier factor based on the average annual temperature of where you live. Heat accelerates the chemical degradation of LFP cells.
thermalStress.info: This value globally affects cycle wear and SEI degradation.

errors.invalidFile:

errors.unexpectedRenderError: The application encountered an unexpected rendering error.

historyMod.title: Trip History
historyMod.description: History keeps a permanent record of all trips you have loaded.

legal.footerRights: Â© 2026 BYD Stats Community. Open Source Project.
legal.footerNote: This page exists for transparency and compliance with Google Play policies.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.summary: Summary: Your data is **yours**. BYD Stats works locally and does not automatically sell or send your information to external servers.

collection.title: 1. Data Collection
collection.text: The application exclusively processes data contained in the EC_Database.db file generated by your BYD vehicle. This data includes trip logs, efficiency, consumption, and locations (start/end GPS coordinates if you save them).

usage.title: 2. Information Usage
usage.text: The information is used solely to generate the statistics, charts, and reports you see on the screen. We do not track users or perform advertising profiling.

storage.title: 3. Storage and Security
storage.local: Local: Data is saved in the local storage of your browser or device (IndexedDB/LocalStorage).
storage.cloud: Cloud (Optional): If you enable Google Drive Synchronization, data will be uploaded to your private Google Drive account (in the "application data" folder). Only you have access to those files.

thirdParty.title: 4. Third Party Services
thirdParty.text: We use Google APIs only for the optional synchronization feature. You can check Google's privacy policy to see how they handle your Google account.

about.title: About the Project
about.text: BYD Stats Analyzer is an Open Source tool developed by the community and for the community. We have no official relationship with BYD Auto Co., Ltd. or its subsidiaries.

trademarks.title: Trademarks
trademarks.text: The names "BYD", vehicle logos, and other trademarks mentioned belong to their respective owners and are used here solely for descriptive and informational purposes.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.text: The application is offered "as is", without warranties of any kind. We are not responsible for any data loss, calculation inaccuracies, or misuse of the information obtained. Data is extracted from files generated by the vehicle software and may contain errors outside this application.

contributions.title: Contributions
contributions.text: If you wish to contribute, report a bug, or review the source code, you can do so in our official GitHub repository.

help.title: Help & Reports
help.subtitle: Project Information
help.description: This application is open source and is made to help the BYD user community.

charges.title: Charging Log
charges.addFirst: Tap + to add your first charge

chargeInsights.cyclesExplanation: One cycle = 100% of battery capacity usage

tripInsights.rangeDesc: This calculation uses your trip history and current battery settings. It is essential to enter the actual battery size and health (SoH) in the settings for accurate data. Actual range may vary based on weather, load, and driving style.
tripInsights.rangeHighwayDesc: Estimated with 20% higher consumption than usual.
tripInsights.rangeCityDesc: Estimated with 20% lower consumption than usual.
tripInsights.stationaryDesc: This accounts for all consumption from registered trips shorter than 0.5km.
tripInsights.sohDesc: Advanced estimation based on natural LFP cell degradation and usage history.
tripInsights.sohSeiDesc: Linear drop of up to 2% during the first 50 cycles.
tripInsights.sohStressDesc: Impact of charging power: Slow 8A (0.9x), AC (1.0x), DC (1.2x), HPC (2.8x).
tripInsights.sohCalendarDesc: Natural aging due to time (0.75% annually).

sync.conflictDescription: There are differences between your local settings and the cloud. What would you like to do?

calendar.title: Activity Calendar
calendar.noActivity: No activity recorded on this day

general.title: General
general.a1: Yes, BYD Stats is 100% free and open source. It was developed as a personal project to help the BYD community better visualize their data.
general.a2: Your data is yours. By default, all information is stored locally on your device. We do not upload anything to external servers nor sell your demographics.
general.q3: Do I need a Google account to use it?
general.a3: No, it is not mandatory. You can use all analysis features without registering. A Google account is only needed if you voluntarily activate "Cloud Sync" to save a backup to your own Google Drive.
general.q_no_efficiency: Why don't I see efficiency or Score for some trips?
general.a_no_efficiency: Trips shorter than 0.5km (0.3mi) do not show efficiency or Score and appear with a hyphen. This is because such short distances with startup or climate control consumption would generate artificially high efficiencies. These are considered 'Stationary Consumption' and are summed separately.
general.a_score_works: Score is not a fixed scale, it is dynamic and adapted to your car. A 10 is the best efficiency achieved in YOUR car for a trip (>0.5km) and 0 is the worst. Two identical cars could have different Scores for the same trip if their usage history varies.
general.q_cost_types: What do the 3 Cost Types in Settings mean?
general.a_cost_avg: Historical average of all your recorded charges.
general.a_cost_dynamic: Price per kW paid on the last charge before the trip.

database.title: Database
database.q1: How do I get the EC_Database.db file from my car?
database.a1_intro: Follow these steps on your BYD screen:
database.a1_step1: Connect a USB drive (FAT32 format) to the car's data USB port.
database.a1_step2: Open the "File Manager" app in the car.
database.a1_step3: Navigate to: Internal Storage > BYD > ElectronicOwnerManual > data.
database.a1_step4: Copy the EC_Database.db file and paste it onto your USB drive.
database.tip_text: If you have trouble selecting the file when uploading, you can rename it to EC_Database.jpg. The application will accept the file as a valid image and process it correctly.
database.q_csv_import: Can I import my own charges/trips?
database.a_csv_import: Yes, you can import your data from CSV files. Download the official templates from GitHub to ensure you use the correct format:

install.title: Installation (PWA)
install.q1: How do I install the App on my phone (Android/iOS)?
install.a1_intro: You will usually see an automatic banner when entering the website inviting you to install the application.
install.a1_ios: Tap the "Share" button and select "Add to Home Screen".
install.a1_android: Tap the menu (three dots) and select "Install App" or "Add to Home Screen".
install.q2: Can I install it on the car screen?
install.a2_intro: Yes, if your car has a web browser (like GBox or native browser):
install.a2_step1: Open bydstats.com in the car browser.
install.a2_step2: Look for the browser option to "Add to Home Screen" or "Install".
install.a2_step3: It will now appear as just another app on your multimedia system.

support.title: Support & Contact
support.q1: How can I support the project?
support.a1: If you find the application useful and want to support its maintenance and ongoing development, you can make a small voluntary donation via Ko-fi. Any contribution is immensely appreciated!
support.q2: Do you have questions or suggestions?
support.a2: For any questions, ideas, contributions, or if you find any issues, feel free to write to me at

bms.title: BMS & LFP Calibration
bms.definition: In LFP batteries, the voltage curve is very flat, making it difficult for the BMS to know the exact energy without reaching the extremes. Therefore, imbalance is common and expected in this technology.
bms.calculation: The warning appears if the BMS detects voltage differences between cells or if it has been a long time without reaching 100%. It needs that reference point (top balancing) to resynchronize.
bms.impact: It is recommended to charge to 100% at least once a month and leave it plugged in for a few extra hours for balancing. LFP batteries are robust and tolerate 100% well, especially if the car is used shortly after balancing finishes.

formula.title: Theoretical SoH Formula
formula.definition:
Mathematical estimation based on LFP cell chemistry and vehicle usage history.

Constants used:
â€¢ SEI: Max 2% (first 50 cycles)
â€¢ Cycles: 0.005% per cycle (adjusted by stress)
â€¢ Time: 0.75% per year
formula.calculation: SoH = 100% - SEI - (Cycles Ã— 0.005% Ã— Stress) - (Years Ã— 0.75%)
formula.impact: This value is a theoretical approximation. The actual SoH may vary, and the car's BMS has the final say on current usable capacity.

ai_soh.title: Real SoH (AI Analysis)
ai_soh.definition: Empirical calculation based on your actual charging sessions. The AI analyzes how much energy really goes into the battery vs. the percentage reported by the car.
ai_soh.calculation: Uses linear regression on your historical charges to find the current 'effective total capacity'. If charging 50% battery requires 30kWh, your total capacity is 60kWh.
ai_soh.impact: This value is more accurate than the theoretical one because it reflects the actual health of your cells. It may be lower than theoretical (if there are unbalanced cells) or higher (if the battery performs better than expected).

samples.title: Quality Samples
samples.definition: Number of charging sessions that the AI has considered valid for its calculation.
samples.calculation: The AI automatically discards small charges (<15-20%), very slow charges, or charges with interruptions, as they introduce a lot of 'noise' and error into the data.
samples.impact: It is normal to have fewer samples than total charges. The more quality samples (deep charges) you have, the more accurate the Real SoH calculation will be.
